info.name: RotaPay API — Auth, Employers, Shifts, Salaries, Clock & Calendar
info.description:
Test collection for RotaPay.

Run order: Register → Verify → Login (saves accessToken) → Employer Create (employerId) → Shift Create (shiftId) → Salary Create (salaryId) → reads/updates → Salary Delete → Shift Delete → Employer Delete.

Model: Shift and Employer are independent; a **Salary** row links them and holds the pay value. Deleting a shift/employer keeps salary rows (the id just becomes null).

Auto-set vars: accessToken, refreshToken, employerId, shiftId, salaryId.

request.description: Verify the server is running before testing auth endpoints.

**Expected:** 201 Created

Creates a new account. An email is sent to `testuser@example.com` with the verification link.

**Note:** Check the server console for the verification token in development mode, or check your email inbox.

**Expected:** 422 Unprocessable Entity

Password is too short and doesn't meet complexity requirements.
**Expected:** 422 Unprocessable Entity

**Expected:** 409 Conflict

Run this AFTER the successful register to confirm duplicate detection.

**Expected:** 200 OK

**How to get the token:**
- Check your email inbox for the verification link
- Or query MongoDB: `db.users.findOne({email:'testuser@example.com'}, {emailVerifyToken:1})`
- Or check server logs (the token is logged in development mode)

The token looks like: `a3f7e2d1b4c9...` (64 hex characters)
**Expected:** 400 Bad Request

**Expected:** 200 OK

Use this before verifying to test the resend flow. A new token will be generated and emailed.

**Expected:** 200 OK (intentionally generic — prevents email enumeration)

Even if the email doesn't exist, we return a success message so attackers can't probe for valid emails.

**Expected:** 200 OK

**Run this AFTER verifying your email.** Returns access and refresh tokens.

Tokens are auto-saved to the collection variables `accessToken` and `refreshToken`.

exec: console.log('✅ Tokens saved to collection variables');

request.description:
**Expected:** 401 Unauthorised

Note the generic error message — doesn't reveal whether the email or password was wrong.

**Expected:** 403 Forbidden

First register this email WITHOUT verifying it, then try to login.

Register body:
```json
{"email":"unverified@example.com","password":"TestPass123"}
```
**Expected:** 422 Unprocessable Entity — password is missing

**Expected:** 200 OK (existing user) or 201 Created (new user)

**How to get a real Google ID token for testing:**
1. In your frontend app, use `@react-oauth/google` or the Google Sign-In JS SDK
2. On successful Google sign-in, you get an `idToken` (also called `credential`)
3. Copy that token here

**Quick test method (browser console):**
```js
google.accounts.id.initialize({
  client_id: 'YOUR_GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID',
  callback: (res) => console.log(res.credential) // This is your idToken
});
```

**Note:** ID tokens expire in ~1 hour. You need a fresh one each test session.
**Expected:** 400 Bad Request

**Expected:** 404 (route not built yet) or 200 once user routes are built.

This tests that the saved `accessToken` is being sent correctly in the Authorization header.

If you get 401, your token may have expired (15 min expiry) — run Login again.
**Expected:** 201. Creates an employer (a.k.a. "employee") for the user. Saves `{{employerId}}`.
**Expected:** 401 — no Authorization header.
**Expected:** 422 — empty store and employer name.
**Expected:** 200. Supports `?isActive=`, `?search=`, `?page=`, `?limit=`.
**Expected:** 200.
**Expected:** 200. Partial update.
**Expected:** 201. Uses startDate/endDate (status + isActive are auto-derived from them). totalHours is client-computed. Optional salaries[] assigns employers. Saves {{shiftId}}.
**Expected:** 422 — endTime before startTime.
**Expected:** 404 — a salary assignment references an employer the user doesn't own.
**Expected:** 200. Filters: ?status=upcoming|isActive|completed, ?search= (notes), ?employerId=, ?shiftType=, ?from=/?to= (startDate), ?confirmed=. meta.summary has total hours.
**Expected:** 200. Demonstrates status filtering (upcoming|isActive|completed) + notes search. status is translated to date conditions server-side.
**Expected:** 200. Totals for the shift dashboard cards: totalHours, thisMonthHours, thisWeekHours, totalPay, thisMonthPay, thisWeekPay. Pay is summed from linked Salary rows by shift date.
**Expected:** 200 — includes the shift's `salaries` array.
**Expected:** 200. Updates shift fields only (salary rows are managed via /api/salaries).
**Expected:** 201. Links a shift + employer with a pay value. Saves `{{salaryId}}`.
**Expected:** 422 — `salary` value is required.
**Expected:** 200. Supports `?shiftId=`, `?employerId=`. `meta.summary.totalSalary` sums the values.
**Expected:** 200.
**Expected:** 201. Starts a session referencing the salary (employer + shift + rate). 409 if already clocked in.
**Expected:** 200. Returns an ARRAY of all currently-active sessions.
**Expected:** 200. Clocks out THIS session (by id). Earnings = clocked hours × salary.hourlyPayRate. Multiple employees can be clocked in at once.
**Expected:** 200. Session history with totals.
**Expected:** 200.
**Expected:** 201. Custom day note (≤15 words), optionally linked to a shift. Saves {{calendarId}}.
**Expected:** 422 — title exceeds 15 words.
**Expected:** 200. Entries within the ?from=/?to= range.
**Expected:** 200.
**Expected:** 200.
**Expected:** 200.
**Expected:** 200. Can change `salary`, or re-link/detach `shiftId`/`employerId` (send null to detach).
**Expected:** 200. Sending null detaches the shift/employer link while KEEPING the salary row and its value (the SetNull behaviour).
**Expected:** 200.
**Expected:** 200. Salary rows are KEPT (their shiftId becomes null).
**Expected:** 200. Shifts are untouched; salary rows are KEPT (employerId becomes null).
